scope: Vox Director concept-video preproduction: required prerequisites, source-art provenance boundaries, and the 36-second Korean beat map.

evidence.subject: Approved-pending 36-second Korean concept-video beat map
evidence.observation: The JSON parses successfully and contains six beats, twelve three-second shots, a hook-payoff arc, post-production captions, GTI-only source-art provenance, and strict flat-collage motion constraints.

evidence.subject: Video generation and accessibility boundary
evidence.observation: The contract permits Vox/Atlas only after credential, terms, GTI source still, local MP4 validation, human motion review, caption/transcript, and static fallback gates are met.

evidence.subject: Local Vox assembly dependencies
evidence.observation: ffmpeg and ffprobe are installed at /opt/homebrew/bin; Python can import Pillow 12.2.0. The required Atlas Cloud credential is not exported in this environment, so no billable remote generation request was submitted.
evidence.command: command -v ffmpeg; command -v ffprobe; python3 -c "import PIL; print(PIL.__version__)"; printenv ATLASCLOUD_API_KEY

evidence.subject: Installed Vox Director pipeline package
evidence.observation: The installed skill contains workflow guidance and reference documents but does not contain the referenced scripts/keyframes.py, clips.py, audio.py, assemble.py, or style_bakeoff.py; no substitute generator was introduced.

findings.statement: Remote Vox generation is not authorized or executable until a release owner supplies the Atlas Cloud credential outside source control and confirms provider output ownership and terms.
The generated beat map is intentionally draft-only because Vox Director requires explicit human approval before expensive keyframe and clip requests.
The installed skill package is missing its documented executable pipeline; a reproducible copy of the upstream scripts must be established before the approved run.

corrective_actions.action: Configure ATLASCLOUD_API_KEY outside the repository and record the provider ownership and retention decision without serializing the credential.
corrective_actions.acceptance: A preflight confirms the credential is available, no secret appears in project files, and the owner has approved the provider terms and output ownership.

corrective_actions.action: Approve or revise the Korean 36-second beat map before any image, clip, voice, or music request.
corrective_actions.acceptance: A human decision identifies the accepted beat-map revision and permits the generation phase.

corrective_actions.action: Establish a reproducible Vox Director script source from the upstream project before the approved run; do not replace it with an unreviewed provider implementation.
corrective_actions.acceptance: The run environment has the documented keyframe, clip, audio, assembly, and style-bakeoff scripts with a recorded source revision.

next_stage_decision.rationale: The concept beat map is complete as a draft, but mandatory human approval, credential/terms evidence, and the documented executable pipeline are missing; generation would violate the provider and provenance gates.
